original_statement: "Netherlands project more than 5.5 goals and sit right at 50% for group win, one of the more attacking group favorites in the draw. Japan are second favorites at 58% to qualify, with Sweden close behind at 44% making this one of the more competitive second-place races in the group stage [RotoWire model, 26 May 2026]."
statement_topic: FIFA World Cup 2026 - Group F second-place qualification probability
statement_context: Excerpt from RotoWire model projections for all 48 teams published on 2026-05-26. Author Adam Zdroik with stated model basis for goals, points and qualification odds.
resolution_criteria: Japan qualifies for the knockout stage from Group F in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, as determined by finishing in the top two positions according to the official FIFA group stage standings published after all group matches conclude on or before 2026-06-26.
source_text_snippet: Japan are second favorites at 58% to qualify, with Sweden close behind at 44% making this one of the more competitive second-place races in the group stage.
